page: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. No GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

This server includes 2835 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8), alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography features like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. These hardware extensions assist in accelerating specialized vector and matrix calculations directly on the CPU.

In benchmark evaluations, the D48ds_v7 demonstrates strong performance across multiple domains. It achieves a PassMark CPU score of 68,963.55 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU performance of 49,841.59. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 3,733.43 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 106.22 GB/sec. Database and caching performance are notable, with 23,458.22 operations per second in PassMark database tests and 84.18 million operations per second in Redis SET operations. Additionally, the instance exhibits top-tier text generation speeds of 332.7 tokens per second for small LLM models.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-speed local storage, the D48ds_v7 offers solid qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense enterprise workloads. The combination of dedicated high-frequency Intel cores, substantial local storage capacity, and advanced vector/matrix instructions makes this server well-suited for relational databases, high-throughput web serving, Redis caching layers, and CPU-based machine learning or LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX for accelerated matrix operations. Benchmarks indicate strong performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 68,963.55, 84.18 million Redis SET operations per second, and 72.52 GB/sec static web serving throughput. It also delivers top-tier text generation speeds of 332.7 tokens per second for small LLM models. Lacking a GPU, this instance is highly cost-efficient for memory-balanced, compute-intensive workloads. It is optimized for database operations, high-throughput web serving, caching layers, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering strong database and CPU-based inference performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage for database and web workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, fast local storage, and advanced matrix acceleration.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets
Strong database performance with 84.18 million Redis SET operations per second
Top-tier CPU-based LLM inference and high-throughput static web serving
Best suited for relational databases, caching layers, and enterprise web applications
